title: Two-color lasers aim electron currents through semiconductor with no electric field
excerpt: Researchers at the University of Michigan have created a device that enables them to control the flow of electrons through a semiconductor using only laser light—no electrical power source required. The device was built to explore fundamental physics and realize a previously unobserved behavior, but it could also open doors for new applications in areas that bridge optics and electronics, including sensing, imaging and telecommunications.
content_html:
Researchers at the University of Michigan have created a device that enables them to control the flow of electrons through a semiconductor using only laser light—no electrical power source required. The device was built to explore fundamental physics and realize a previously unobserved behavior, but it could also open doors for new applications in areas that bridge optics and electronics, including sensing, imaging and telecommunications.

The paper is published in the journal Physical Review Letters.

The phenomenon could help improve how signals are sent through and between devices, as well as create new opportunities to store more information in those signals.

"This electrical device that we manufactured at the Lurie Nanofabrication Facility has the potential to turn into something that measures different aspects of light," said Yiming Gong, who helped lead the project as a doctoral student in the U-M Department of Physics. "But this originates from a very fundamental level of physics, which is the interference between different optical absorption processes."

Steering electrons with light

The researchers showed they could induce the orderly flow of electrons through a semiconductor using two different colors of light. By rotating the polarization of the two optical fields—the direction that the light waves wiggle—the researchers could also control the direction of the electronic current.

"This isn't the way things normally work. When you think about electrons moving through a material, they're moving because you've applied an electric field and they actually bounce around and drift across the materials. Here, using light, you can actually sort of squirt the electrons in a specific direction without applying an electric field," said U-M physicist Steven Cundiff, senior author of the team's PRL report.

Although previous work had shown that light alone could get electrons flowing, this work goes a step further and shows that light gets electrons flowing in a narrow beam in a specific direction.

"The light no longer merely switches the current on; it also aims it," Cundiff said.

Cundiff likens this to a lighthouse, which sweeps a beam of light by rotating its lamp. Here, the beam is made of electrons, and it can be rotated by rotating the polarization of the two phase-coherent optical fields.

How quantum interference directs the beam

The phenomenon relies on quantum interference, which arises when two colors of light drive different absorption pathways to the same final state. The light transfers energy to the semiconductor material in discrete, or quantized, packets called photons, which mobilize the material's electric charge carriers.

In the U-M setup, the incident light is absorbed by two routes at once, and the routes can be thought of as overlapping ripples, Cundiff said. Those ripples line up and enhance each other for electrons traveling in a certain direction but cancel each other out for electrons moving in other directions.

Building the 'electron lighthouse'

A collaborator on an earlier project, J.E. Sipe of the University of Toronto, had predicted it would be possible to create such an "electron lighthouse." Now, working with the Lurie Nanofabrication Facility, Gong was able to realize such a device.

"The LNF is an amazing facility," Gong said, adding that it was a painstaking process to meld the device's materials together in a way that didn't introduce any extraneous electric fields.

"That was the biggest puzzle to solve for me, because there isn't a standard way to do that. So I worked with the LNF staff to play around with different recipes and temperatures to come up with a manufacturing process."

Kai Wang, a former postdoctoral scientist with Cundiff and now a professor of electronics and information at Sun Yat-Sen University in China, provided Gong with additional guidance on this project.

Gong has earned his doctorate from U-M and now works in Chicago as a machine-learning scientist, where he applies the quantitative and analytical skills developed through his physics research to machine learning.

Publication details

Yiming Gong et al, Directional Photocurrent Generated by Quantum Interference Control, Physical Review Letters (2026). DOI: 10.1103/3v91-5pzf. On arXiv: DOI: 10.48550/arxiv.2511.05318

Who's behind this story?

Sadie Harley

BSc Life Sciences & Ecology. Microbiology lab background with pharmaceutical news experience in oil, gas, and renewable industries. Full profile →

content_text: When two pulses of different colored lasers (the two waves at the top of the image) meet in a new device created at the University of Michigan, researchers create a beam of electrons (small golden particles) that flows in a controllable direction. By changing the laser colors, the electron beam can sweep through different directions like the beam of a lighthouse. Credit: Yiming Gong Researchers at the University of Michigan have created a device that enables them to control the flow of electrons through a semiconductor using only laser light—no electrical power source required. The device was built to explore fundamental physics and realize a previously unobserved behavior, but it could also open doors for new applications in areas that bridge optics and electronics, including sensing, imaging and telecommunications. The paper is published in the journal Physical Review Letters. The phenomenon could help improve how signals are sent through and between devices, as well as create new opportunities to store more information in those signals. "This electrical device that we manufactured at the Lurie Nanofabrication Facility has the potential to turn into something that measures different aspects of light," said Yiming Gong, who helped lead the project as a doctoral student in the U-M Department of Physics. "But this originates from a very fundamental level of physics, which is the interference between different optical absorption processes." Steering electrons with light The researchers showed they could induce the orderly flow of electrons through a semiconductor using two different colors of light. By rotating the polarization of the two optical fields—the direction that the light waves wiggle—the researchers could also control the direction of the electronic current. When you think about electrons moving through a material, they're moving because you've applied an electric field and they actually bounce around and drift across the materials. Here, using light, you can actually sort of squirt the electrons in a specific direction without applying an electric field," said U-M physicist Steven Cundiff, senior author of the team's PRL report. Although previous work had shown that light alone could get electrons flowing, this work goes a step further and shows that light gets electrons flowing in a narrow beam in a specific direction. Here, the beam is made of electrons, and it can be rotated by rotating the polarization of the two phase-coherent optical fields. The light transfers energy to the semiconductor material in discrete, or quantized, packets called photons, which mobilize the material's electric charge carriers. sentences.en: When two pulses of different colored lasers (the two waves at the top of the image) meet in a new device created at the University of Michigan, researchers create a beam of electrons (small golden particles) that flows in a controllable direction.
sentences.zh: 当不同颜色激光的两个脉冲（图像顶部的两个波）在密歇根大学创建的新设备中相遇时，研究人员会产生一束沿可控方向流动的电子束（金色小粒子）。

sentences.en: By changing the laser colors, the electron beam can sweep through different directions like the beam of a lighthouse.
sentences.zh: 通过改变激光颜色，电子束可以像灯塔的光束一样扫过不同的方向。

sentences.en: Credit: Yiming Gong

blocks.html_en: Researchers at the University of Michigan have created a device that enables them to control the flow of electrons through a semiconductor using only laser light—no electrical power source required. The device was built to explore fundamental physics and realize a previously unobserved behavior, but it could also open doors for new applications in areas that bridge optics and electronics, including sensing, imaging and telecommunications.

sentences.en: Researchers at the University of Michigan have created a device that enables them to control the flow of electrons through a semiconductor using only laser light—no electrical power source required.

sentences.en: The device was built to explore fundamental physics and realize a previously unobserved behavior, but it could also open doors for new applications in areas that bridge optics and electronics, including sensing, imaging and telecommunications.
sentences.zh: 该设备旨在探索基础物理并实现以前未观察到的行为，但它也可以为连接光学和电子学领域的新应用打开大门，包括传感、成像和电信。

sentences.en: "But this originates from a very fundamental level of physics, which is the interference between different optical absorption processes."
sentences.zh: “但这源于物理学的一个非常基本的层面，即不同光学吸收过程之间的干扰。”

blocks.html_en: Steering electrons with light

sentences.en: Steering electrons with light
sentences.zh: 用光控制电子

blocks.html_en: The researchers showed they could induce the orderly flow of electrons through a semiconductor using two different colors of light. By rotating the polarization of the two optical fields—the direction that the light waves wiggle—the researchers could also control the direction of the electronic current.

sentences.en: The researchers showed they could induce the orderly flow of electrons through a semiconductor using two different colors of light.
sentences.zh: 研究人员表明，他们可以使用两种不同颜色的光诱导电子通过半导体有序流动。

sentences.en: By rotating the polarization of the two optical fields—the direction that the light waves wiggle—the researchers could also control the direction of the electronic current.
sentences.zh: 通过旋转两个光场的偏振（光波摆动的方向），研究人员还可以控制电子电流的方向。

blocks.html_en: "This isn't the way things normally work. When you think about electrons moving through a material, they're moving because you've applied an electric field and they actually bounce around and drift across the materials. Here, using light, you can actually sort of squirt the electrons in a specific direction without applying an electric field," said U-M physicist Steven Cundiff, senior author of the team's PRL report.

sentences.en: "This isn't the way things normally work.
sentences.zh: “事情通常不是这样进行的。

sentences.en: When you think about electrons moving through a material, they're moving because you've applied an electric field and they actually bounce around and drift across the materials.
sentences.zh: 当您考虑电子在材料中移动时，它们会移动，因为您施加了电场，并且它们实际上在材料上反弹并漂移。

sentences.en: Here, using light, you can actually sort of squirt the electrons in a specific direction without applying an electric field," said U-M physicist Steven Cundiff, senior author of the team's PRL report.
sentences.zh: 在这里，利用光，你实际上可以在不施加电场的情况下向特定方向喷射电子，”该团队 PRL 报告的高级作者、密歇根大学物理学家 Steven Cundiff 说。

blocks.html_en: Although previous work had shown that light alone could get electrons flowing, this work goes a step further and shows that light gets electrons flowing in a narrow beam in a specific direction.

sentences.en: Although previous work had shown that light alone could get electrons flowing, this work goes a step further and shows that light gets electrons flowing in a narrow beam in a specific direction.
sentences.zh: 尽管之前的工作表明光可以单独使电子流动，但这项工作更进一步表明光可以使电子在特定方向上以窄光束流动。

blocks.html_en: "The light no longer merely switches the current on; it also aims it," Cundiff said.

sentences.en: "The light no longer merely switches the current on; it also aims it," Cundiff said.
sentences.zh: 坎迪夫说：“灯不再仅仅打开电流；它还瞄准电流。”

blocks.html_en: Cundiff likens this to a lighthouse, which sweeps a beam of light by rotating its lamp. Here, the beam is made of electrons, and it can be rotated by rotating the polarization of the two phase-coherent optical fields.

sentences.en: Cundiff likens this to a lighthouse, which sweeps a beam of light by rotating its lamp.
sentences.zh: 坎迪夫将其比作一座灯塔，通过旋转灯来扫射光束。

sentences.en: Here, the beam is made of electrons, and it can be rotated by rotating the polarization of the two phase-coherent optical fields.
sentences.zh: 这里，光束由电子组成，可以通过旋转两个相位相干光场的偏振来旋转光束。

blocks.html_en: How quantum interference directs the beam

sentences.en: How quantum interference directs the beam
sentences.zh: 量子干涉如何引导光束

blocks.html_en: The phenomenon relies on quantum interference, which arises when two colors of light drive different absorption pathways to the same final state. The light transfers energy to the semiconductor material in discrete, or quantized, packets called photons, which mobilize the material's electric charge carriers.

sentences.en: The phenomenon relies on quantum interference, which arises when two colors of light drive different absorption pathways to the same final state.
sentences.zh: 这种现象依赖于量子干涉，当两种颜色的光驱动不同的吸收路径达到相同的最终状态时，就会出现量子干涉。

sentences.en: The light transfers energy to the semiconductor material in discrete, or quantized, packets called photons, which mobilize the material's electric charge carriers.
sentences.zh: 光以离散或量子化的光子包形式将能量传输到半导体材料，从而动员材料的电荷载流子。

blocks.html_en: In the U-M setup, the incident light is absorbed by two routes at once, and the routes can be thought of as overlapping ripples, Cundiff said. Those ripples line up and enhance each other for electrons traveling in a certain direction but cancel each other out for electrons moving in other directions.

sentences.en: In the U-M setup, the incident light is absorbed by two routes at once, and the routes can be thought of as overlapping ripples, Cundiff said.
sentences.zh: 坎迪夫说，在密歇根大学的设置中，入射光同时被两条路径吸收，并且这些路径可以被认为是重叠的波纹。

sentences.en: Those ripples line up and enhance each other for electrons traveling in a certain direction but cancel each other out for electrons moving in other directions.
sentences.zh: 对于沿特定方向移动的电子，这些波纹排列并相互增强，但对于沿其他方向移动的电子，这些波纹相互抵消。

blocks.html_en: Building the 'electron lighthouse'

sentences.en: Building the 'electron lighthouse'
sentences.zh: 建造“电子灯塔”

blocks.html_en: A collaborator on an earlier project, J.E. Sipe of the University of Toronto, had predicted it would be possible to create such an "electron lighthouse." Now, working with the Lurie Nanofabrication Facility, Gong was able to realize such a device.

sentences.en: A collaborator on an earlier project, J.E.
sentences.zh: 早期项目的合作者 J.E.

sentences.en: Sipe of the University of Toronto, had predicted it would be possible to create such an "electron lighthouse."
sentences.zh: 多伦多大学的 Sipe 曾预测建造这样一座“电子灯塔”是可能的。

sentences.en: Now, working with the Lurie Nanofabrication Facility, Gong was able to realize such a device.
sentences.zh: 现在，通过与卢里纳米加工设施合作，Gong 能够实现这样的设备。

blocks.html_en: "The LNF is an amazing facility," Gong said, adding that it was a painstaking process to meld the device's materials together in a way that didn't introduce any extraneous electric fields.

sentences.en: "The LNF is an amazing facility," Gong said, adding that it was a painstaking process to meld the device's materials together in a way that didn't introduce any extraneous electric fields.
sentences.zh: “LNF 是一个令人惊叹的设施，”Gong 说，并补充说，以不引入任何外部电场的方式将该设备的材料融合在一起是一个艰苦的过程。

blocks.html_en: "That was the biggest puzzle to solve for me, because there isn't a standard way to do that. So I worked with the LNF staff to play around with different recipes and temperatures to come up with a manufacturing process."

sentences.en: "That was the biggest puzzle to solve for me, because there isn't a standard way to do that.
sentences.zh: “这是我要解决的最大难题，因为没有标准的方法可以做到这一点。

sentences.en: So I worked with the LNF staff to play around with different recipes and temperatures to come up with a manufacturing process."
sentences.zh: 因此，我与 LNF 工作人员合作，尝试不同的配方和温度来制定制造工艺。”

blocks.html_en: Kai Wang, a former postdoctoral scientist with Cundiff and now a professor of electronics and information at Sun Yat-Sen University in China, provided Gong with additional guidance on this project.

sentences.en: Kai Wang, a former postdoctoral scientist with Cundiff and now a professor of electronics and information at Sun Yat-Sen University in China, provided Gong with additional guidance on this project.
sentences.zh: 前康迪夫大学博士后科学家、现任中国中山大学电子与信息学教授王凯为龚先生提供了有关该项目的额外指导。

blocks.html_en: Gong has earned his doctorate from U-M and now works in Chicago as a machine-learning scientist, where he applies the quantitative and analytical skills developed through his physics research to machine learning.

sentences.en: Gong has earned his doctorate from U-M and now works in Chicago as a machine-learning scientist, where he applies the quantitative and analytical skills developed through his physics research to machine learning.
sentences.zh: 龚获得了密歇根大学的博士学位，现在在芝加哥担任机器学习科学家，他将通过物理研究开发的定量和分析技能应用到机器学习中。

content_html_zh:
密歇根大学的研究人员发明了一种装置，使他们能够仅使用激光来控制电子在半导体中的流动，无需电源。 该设备旨在探索基础物理并实现以前未观察到的行为，但它也可以为连接光学和电子学领域的新应用打开大门，包括传感、成像和电信。

该论文发表在《物理评论快报》杂志上。

这种现象可能有助于改善信号在设备之间发送的方式，并为在这些信号中存储更多信息创造新的机会。

“我们在卢里纳米加工设施制造的这种电子设备有潜力变成测量光的不同方面的东西，”密歇根大学物理系博士生龚一鸣说。 “但这源于物理学的一个非常基本的层面，即不同光学吸收过程之间的干扰。”

用光控制电子

研究人员表明，他们可以使用两种不同颜色的光诱导电子通过半导体有序流动。 通过旋转两个光场的偏振（光波摆动的方向），研究人员还可以控制电子电流的方向。

“事情通常不是这样进行的。 当您考虑电子在材料中移动时，它们会移动，因为您施加了电场，并且它们实际上在材料上反弹并漂移。 在这里，利用光，你实际上可以在不施加电场的情况下向特定方向喷射电子，”该团队 PRL 报告的高级作者、密歇根大学物理学家 Steven Cundiff 说。

尽管之前的工作表明光可以单独使电子流动，但这项工作更进一步表明光可以使电子在特定方向上以窄光束流动。

坎迪夫说：“灯不再仅仅打开电流；它还瞄准电流。”

坎迪夫将其比作一座灯塔，通过旋转灯来扫射光束。 这里，光束由电子组成，可以通过旋转两个相位相干光场的偏振来旋转光束。

量子干涉如何引导光束

这种现象依赖于量子干涉，当两种颜色的光驱动不同的吸收路径达到相同的最终状态时，就会出现量子干涉。 光以离散或量子化的光子包形式将能量传输到半导体材料，从而动员材料的电荷载流子。

坎迪夫说，在密歇根大学的设置中，入射光同时被两条路径吸收，并且这些路径可以被认为是重叠的波纹。 对于沿特定方向移动的电子，这些波纹排列并相互增强，但对于沿其他方向移动的电子，这些波纹相互抵消。

建造“电子灯塔”

早期项目的合作者 J.E. 多伦多大学的 Sipe 曾预测建造这样一座“电子灯塔”是可能的。 现在，通过与卢里纳米加工设施合作，Gong 能够实现这样的设备。

“LNF 是一个令人惊叹的设施，”Gong 说，并补充说，以不引入任何外部电场的方式将该设备的材料融合在一起是一个艰苦的过程。

“这是我要解决的最大难题，因为没有标准的方法可以做到这一点。 因此，我与 LNF 工作人员合作，尝试不同的配方和温度来制定制造工艺。”

前康迪夫大学博士后科学家、现任中国中山大学电子与信息学教授王凯为龚先生提供了有关该项目的额外指导。

龚获得了密歇根大学的博士学位，现在在芝加哥担任机器学习科学家，他将通过物理研究开发的定量和分析技能应用到机器学习中。
